feature_list.test_description: Traceability for absence of combinatorial paths between interface input and output signals.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement does not identify specific interface signals, paths, or an observable dynamic method to prove absence of combinatorial logic from the provided information.
test_steps: Identify the specific input and output signals and the interface instance to be checked.
Define the verification method or structural analysis criteria needed to prove no combinatorial path exists.
expected_results: Verification is blocked until signal scope and analysis method are defined.
feature_list.requirement_text: There must be no combinatorial paths between input and output signals on an interface.

feature_list.test_description: Traceability for use of a single active-LOW reset signal ARESETn.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement names ARESETn but does not provide observable interface instances, reset mapping, or configuration context needed to verify that it is the single reset used.
test_steps: Identify the reset ports/signals implemented on the AXI interface.
Confirm which reset signal is designated as the protocol reset and whether any additional reset signals exist.
expected_results: Verification is blocked until the implemented reset interface and observability are defined.
feature_list.requirement_text: A2.1.2 Reset The AXI protocol uses a single active-LOW reset signal, ARESETn.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that reset deassertion occurs only synchronously with a rising edge of ACLK.
feature_list.test_constraints: Asynchronous assertion is optional per edge case; only synchronous deassertion is mandatory and directly testable from the requirement.
test_steps: Assert reset, then deassert ARESETn at a non-rising-clock time relative to ACLK.
Observe when protocol-visible reset release takes effect.
expected_results: Reset release does not take effect until a rising edge of ACLK.
No earlier deassertion is recognized between rising edges.
feature_list.requirement_text: The reset signal can be asserted asynchronously, but deassertion can only be synchronous with a rising edge of ACLK.

feature_list.test_description: Traceability for optional asynchronous reset assertion capability.
feature_list.test_constraints: Edge case states asynchronous assertion is not mandatory; requirement permits but does not require implementations to support asynchronous assertion.
test_steps: Determine whether the implementation claims support for asynchronous reset assertion.
If supported, define the observable reset-entry behavior and timing needed for verification.
expected_results: Asynchronous assertion support is implementation-dependent from the provided information.
feature_list.requirement_text: The reset signal can be asserted asynchronously, but deassertion can only be synchronous with a rising edge of ACLK.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that signals required to be deasserted during reset remain deasserted until at least the rising ACLK edge after ARESETn is HIGH.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement does not identify which specific signals are required to be deasserted during reset; applies only to those signals once identified.
test_steps: Hold a reset-deasserted-during-reset signal LOW while ARESETn is asserted, then drive ARESETn HIGH before a rising ACLK edge.
Observe the signal through the first rising ACLK edge after ARESETn is HIGH.
expected_results: The signal remains deasserted until at least the first rising ACLK edge after ARESETn goes HIGH.
No earlier assertion occurs before that edge.
feature_list.requirement_text: Signals that are required to be deasserted during reset must remain deasserted at least until the rising ACLK edge after ARESETn is HIGH.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that a signal required to be deasserted during reset is not asserted before a rising ACLK edge after ARESETn is HIGH.
feature_list.test_constraints: Edge case notes the requirement defines only the earliest legal assertion point, not that assertion must occur on the first eligible edge; specific applicable signals are not identified.
test_steps: Drive ARESETn HIGH between rising edges of ACLK while keeping a reset-deasserted signal LOW.
Observe whether the signal asserts before the next rising ACLK edge.
expected_results: The signal does not assert before a rising ACLK edge after ARESETn is HIGH.
feature_list.requirement_text: The earliest point these signals can be asserted is at a rising ACLK edge after ARESETn is HIGH.

feature_list.test_description: Traceability for matching AXI_Transport property values on connected Manager and Subordinate interfaces.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement refers to AXI_Transport property values, but no property encoding, access mechanism, or connected interface instances are provided.
test_steps: Identify the connected Manager and Subordinate interfaces and how AXI_Transport is represented.
Define the allowed property values and how equality is to be checked.
expected_results: Verification is blocked until property definition and interface configuration are provided.
feature_list.requirement_text: Connected Manager and Subordinate interfaces must have the same value for the AXI_Transport property.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that VALID signals are LOW during reset.
feature_list.test_constraints: Applies to each VALID signal present on the interface; specific channel instances are not enumerated in the requirement.
test_steps: Assert reset.
Observe each implemented VALID signal during reset.
expected_results: Each VALID signal remains LOW while reset is asserted.
feature_list.requirement_text: The Manager must not wait for the Subordinate to assert ARREADY before asserting ARVALID.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that the transmitter keeps its information stable until the transfer occurs when the VALID assertion is recognized.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement references 'information' and a transfer event but does not identify the exact signal set for the information payload/control.
test_steps: Assert VALID with associated information while READY is not completing the transfer.
Observe the information until the handshake occurs.
expected_results: The transmitted information remains stable until the transfer occurs.
The transfer is recognized only at the handshake edge.
feature_list.requirement_text: The Subordinate must wait for both ARVALID and ARREADY to be asserted before it asserts RVALID to indicate that valid data is available.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that the transmitter does not wait for READY before asserting VALID.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires stimulus that keeps READY deasserted while the transmitter has data to send; exact transmitter-side request trigger is not specified.
test_steps: Hold READY LOW while making data available for transmission.
Observe whether VALID is asserted before READY goes HIGH.
expected_results: VALID is asserted without waiting for READY to be asserted.
feature_list.requirement_text: The Subordinate must not wait for the Manager to assert RREADY before asserting RVALID.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that once VALID is asserted, it remains asserted until a rising-edge handshake with READY occurs.
feature_list.test_constraints: Assumes a clocked handshake observation point; applies per VALID/READY channel pair.
test_steps: Assert VALID while READY is LOW.
Observe VALID until a rising clock edge where VALID and READY are both HIGH.
expected_results: VALID remains asserted before the handshake edge.
Handshake completes only at a rising clock edge with VALID and READY both asserted.
feature_list.requirement_text: The Subordinate must wait for the last write data transfer before asserting BVALID.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that the receiver may wait for VALID before asserting the corresponding READY.
feature_list.test_constraints: Edge case identifies this as permitted rather than mandatory behaviour; verifying this legal mode does not prove READY must wait in all cases.
test_steps: Keep READY LOW before VALID is asserted.
Assert VALID and then allow the receiver to assert READY afterward.
expected_results: A sequence with READY asserted after VALID is accepted as legal behaviour.
feature_list.requirement_text: The Subordinate must not wait for the Manager to assert BREADY before asserting BVALID.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that READY may be deasserted before VALID is asserted when READY had been asserted earlier.
feature_list.test_constraints: Permitted conditional behaviour only; edge case notes transient READY pulse with no transfer is legal.
test_steps: Assert READY while VALID is LOW.
Deassert READY before VALID is asserted and observe for transfer activity.
expected_results: READY may return LOW before VALID is asserted.
No handshake occurs while VALID remains LOW.
feature_list.requirement_text: If READY is asserted, it is permitted to deassert READY before VALID is asserted.

feature_list.test_description: Traceability for requirement that the Subordinate must be able to accept any valid request that is presented to it.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement lacks definition of 'any valid request', acceptance criteria, channels, and observable outcomes.
test_steps: Define the set of requests considered valid for the interface implementation.
Define the observable acceptance condition for each request type.
expected_results: Verification is blocked until valid request space and acceptance observables are defined.
feature_list.requirement_text: The Manager must not wait for the Subordinate to assert ARREADY before asserting ARVALID.

feature_list.test_description: Traceability for dependency indicated by double-headed arrows requiring assertion ordering.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement refers to a diagrammatic notation not included in the input; the actual signal pairs and ordering dependencies are missing.
test_steps: Provide the referenced diagram or enumerate each arrow-defined signal dependency.
Define the assertion ordering to be checked for each dependency.
expected_results: Verification is blocked until the referenced arrow relationships are supplied.
feature_list.requirement_text: The Manager must not wait for the Subordinate to assert ARREADY before asserting ARVALID.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that the Manager does not wait for AWREADY before asserting AWVALID.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a write request pending while AWREADY is held LOW.
test_steps: Hold AWREADY LOW while a write address request is pending.
Observe whether AWVALID is asserted before AWREADY goes HIGH.
expected_results: AWVALID is asserted without waiting for AWREADY.
feature_list.requirement_text: The Subordinate must wait for both ARVALID and ARREADY to be asserted before it asserts RVALID to indicate that valid data is available.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that the Manager does not wait for WREADY before asserting WVALID.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires write data pending while WREADY is held LOW.
test_steps: Hold WREADY LOW while write data is pending.
Observe whether WVALID is asserted before WREADY goes HIGH.
expected_results: WVALID is asserted without waiting for WREADY.
feature_list.requirement_text: The Subordinate must wait for both ARVALID and ARREADY to be asserted before it asserts RVALID to indicate that valid data is available.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that the Subordinate asserts BVALID only after AWVALID, AWREADY, WVALID, and WREADY have all been asserted.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement gives prerequisite signals but does not state whether they must occur in one cycle or over prior handshakes; assertion ordering is still observable.
test_steps: Generate a write transaction and delay one prerequisite handshake condition at a time.
Observe when BVALID is first asserted.
expected_results: BVALID is not asserted before AWVALID, AWREADY, WVALID, and WREADY have all been asserted.
BVALID can assert only after the required prerequisites have occurred.
feature_list.requirement_text: The Subordinate must not wait for the Manager to assert RREADY before asserting RVALID.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that the Subordinate waits for the last write data transfer before asserting BVALID.
feature_list.test_constraints: Applies when write data has a defined last transfer indication, but that indication is not named in the requirement text.
test_steps: Issue a multi-beat write transaction and prevent the final write data transfer from completing.
Observe BVALID before and after the last write data transfer.
expected_results: BVALID is not asserted before the last write data transfer occurs.
BVALID may assert after the last write data transfer.
feature_list.requirement_text: The Subordinate must wait for the last write data transfer before asserting BVALID.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that the Subordinate does not wait for BREADY before asserting BVALID.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a completed write response condition while BREADY is held LOW.
test_steps: Hold BREADY LOW after the write response becomes due.
Observe whether BVALID is asserted before BREADY goes HIGH.
expected_results: BVALID is asserted without waiting for BREADY.
feature_list.requirement_text: The Subordinate must not wait for the Manager to assert BREADY before asserting BVALID.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that the Manager does not wait for ARREADY before asserting ARVALID.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a read request pending while ARREADY is held LOW.
test_steps: Hold ARREADY LOW while a read address request is pending.
Observe whether ARVALID is asserted before ARREADY goes HIGH.
expected_results: ARVALID is asserted without waiting for ARREADY.
feature_list.requirement_text: The Manager must not wait for the Subordinate to assert ARREADY before asserting ARVALID.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that the Subordinate asserts RVALID only after both ARVALID and ARREADY have been asserted.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement covers availability indication only; data content and response timing beyond prerequisite assertion are not specified.
test_steps: Issue a read transaction while delaying ARVALID or ARREADY assertion.
Observe when RVALID is first asserted.
expected_results: RVALID is not asserted before both ARVALID and ARREADY have been asserted.
RVALID may assert after both prerequisites are asserted.
feature_list.requirement_text: The Subordinate must wait for both ARVALID and ARREADY to be asserted before it asserts RVALID to indicate that valid data is available.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that the Subordinate does not wait for RREADY before asserting RVALID.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires read data available while RREADY is held LOW.
test_steps: Hold RREADY LOW when read data becomes available.
Observe whether RVALID is asserted before RREADY goes HIGH.
expected_results: RVALID is asserted without waiting for RREADY.
feature_list.requirement_text: The Subordinate must not wait for the Manager to assert RREADY before asserting RVALID.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that all CRDT and CRDTSH signals are LOW.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement does not specify operating state or timeframe; interpreted as a static protocol rule for implemented CRDT and CRDTSH signals.
test_steps: Observe each implemented CRDT and CRDTSH signal.
Check their driven values.
expected_results: All observed CRDT signals are LOW.
All observed CRDTSH signals are LOW.
feature_list.requirement_text: All CRDT and CRDTSH signals must be LOW.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that the channel transmitter uses one credit in each cycle that VALID is asserted.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires visibility of transmitter credit count or equivalent observable credit accounting; such observability is not defined in the input.
test_steps: Identify how transmitter credit usage is observed or measured.
Drive VALID for one or more cycles and monitor corresponding credit consumption.
expected_results: One credit is consumed in each cycle that VALID is asserted.
feature_list.requirement_text: The channel transmitter uses a credit each cycle that VALID is asserted.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that VALID is not asserted when the channel transmitter has zero credits.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a controllable or observable zero-credit condition; credit state visibility is not specified in the input.
test_steps: Drive the transmitter to a zero-credit condition.
Observe whether VALID is asserted while credits remain at zero.
expected_results: VALID is not asserted when the transmitter has zero credits.
feature_list.requirement_text: VALID must not be asserted when the channel transmitter has zero credits.

feature_list.test_description: Traceability for absence of combinatorial paths between credit signals and other channel signals in either direction.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement does not identify channel signals, implementation structure, or an observable method to prove absence of combinatorial paths.
test_steps: Identify the credit signals and the other channel signals in scope.
Define the structural or timing analysis needed to prove no combinatorial paths in either direction.
expected_results: Verification is blocked until signal scope and proof method are defined.
feature_list.requirement_text: There must not be combinatorial paths between credit signals and other signals on a channel in either direction.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that a credit given in a cycle cannot be used for a transfer in that same cycle.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires observability of the cycle in which a credit is given and the cycle in which a transfer consumes credit; exact credit-give signal is not identified.
test_steps: Cause a credit to be given in a cycle.
Check whether a transfer uses that credit in the same cycle.
expected_results: No transfer uses a newly given credit in the same cycle it is given.
feature_list.requirement_text: A credit cannot be used for a transfer in the same cycle that it is given.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that a credit cannot be given in the same cycle that it is used by a transfer.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires observability of both credit-give and credit-use events in cycle alignment; exact signals are not provided.
test_steps: Create a cycle in which a transfer uses a credit.
Check whether a credit-give event also occurs in that same cycle.
expected_results: A credit is not given in the same cycle that a transfer uses a credit.
feature_list.requirement_text: A credit cannot be given in the same cycle that it is used by a transfer.

feature_list.test_description: Traceability for example-specific statement that cycle 4 uses a credit for a transfer.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement is example-specific and lacks the preceding scenario definition, cycle numbering reference, and observable setup needed to reproduce 'cycle 4'.
test_steps: Provide the full example waveform or scenario that defines cycle numbering and prior credit state.
Define the observable transfer and credit events for cycle 4.
expected_results: Verification is blocked until the referenced example context is provided.
feature_list.requirement_text: Cycle 4 The Tx uses the credit for a transfer.

feature_list.test_description: Traceability for example-specific statement that cycle 5 uses the second credit for a transfer.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement is example-specific and lacks the prior scenario, credit sequence, and cycle numbering needed to verify 'cycle 5' and 'second credit'.
test_steps: Provide the full example waveform or scenario that defines cycle numbering and prior credit issuance/use.
Define the observable event corresponding to use of the second credit on cycle 5.
expected_results: Verification is blocked until the referenced example context is provided.
feature_list.requirement_text: Cycle 5 The Tx uses the second credit for a transfer.

feature_list.test_description: Traceability for example-specific statement that cycle 8 uses a credit for a transfer.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement is example-specific and lacks the full scenario, cycle numbering reference, and prior credit state needed to verify 'cycle 8'.
test_steps: Provide the full example waveform or scenario that defines cycle numbering and credit state through cycle 8.
Define the observable transfer and credit-use event for cycle 8.
expected_results: Verification is blocked until the referenced example context is provided.
feature_list.requirement_text: Cycle 8 The Tx uses the credit for a transfer.

feature_list.test_description: Verify VALID is delayed by the same number of pipeline cycles as the channel payload signals.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a channel configuration with observable payload signals and known pipeline stages. RP and SHAREDCRD are only applicable if present.
test_steps: Apply a transaction on a channel with known payload timing and observe payload pipeline delay.
Observe the corresponding VALID timing through the same path.
Compare VALID delay against payload signal delay for the channel.
expected_results: VALID is delayed by the same number of cycles as the payload signals.
The VALID-to-payload alignment is preserved at the output of the pipeline.
feature_list.requirement_text: VALID signals must be pipelined by the same number of cycles as the payload signals of that channel including RP and SHAREDCRD signals, if present.

feature_list.test_description: Verify SHAREDCRD is delayed consistently with VALID when SHAREDCRD is present on the channel.
feature_list.test_constraints: Applicable only if SHAREDCRD is implemented on the channel. Exact channel and pipeline configuration must be available.
test_steps: Configure or select a channel instance where SHAREDCRD is present and pipelined.
Drive activity that propagates SHAREDCRD and VALID through the pipeline.
Compare the observed cycle delay of VALID and SHAREDCRD on that channel.
expected_results: VALID and SHAREDCRD are delayed by the same number of pipeline cycles on the channel.
Their timing relationship is preserved after pipelining.
feature_list.requirement_text: VALID signals must be pipelined by the same number of cycles as the payload signals of that channel including RP and SHAREDCRD signals, if present.

feature_list.test_description: Verify PENDING is HIGH in the cycle immediately before VALID is HIGH.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires observable PENDING and VALID timing on the same interface. No additional timing exceptions are specified.
test_steps: Drive or observe a transfer where VALID is asserted.
Check the value of PENDING in the cycle immediately preceding VALID assertion.
Repeat across representative transfers if multiple opportunities exist.
expected_results: PENDING is HIGH in the cycle before VALID is HIGH.
The required one-cycle relationship is preserved.
feature_list.requirement_text: PENDING signals must retain the relationship that they are HIGH in the cycle before VALID is HIGH.

feature_list.test_description: Verify a write response follows the last write transfer of a write transaction.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires identifiable write transaction boundaries and observable write response signaling.
test_steps: Issue a write transaction with a defined final write transfer.
Observe completion of the last write transfer for that transaction.
Observe the subsequent write response for the same transaction.
expected_results: A write response occurs after the last write transfer.
The response does not precede the final write transfer of that transaction.
feature_list.requirement_text: A write response must always follow the last write transfer in a write transaction.

feature_list.test_description: Verify read data follows the read request.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires observable read request and read data association for the same transaction.
test_steps: Issue a read request.
Observe the corresponding read data timing for that transaction.
Check that read data occurs after the read request.
expected_results: Read data occurs only after the read request.
The ordering from request to read data is preserved.
feature_list.requirement_text: Read data and responses must always follow the read request.

feature_list.test_description: Verify read response follows the read request.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires observable read request and response association for the same transaction.
test_steps: Issue a read request.
Observe the corresponding read response timing for that transaction.
Check that the response occurs after the read request.
expected_results: Read response occurs only after the read request.
The ordering from request to response is preserved.
feature_list.requirement_text: Read data and responses must always follow the read request.

feature_list.test_description: Verify a Manager can provide all write data for a transaction without dependency on other transactions from that Manager.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a controllable Manager capable of issuing multiple transactions and observable write-data progress. Dependency conditions are only indirectly testable from interface behavior.
test_steps: Issue a write request from the Manager and begin providing all write data for that transaction.
Create another transaction context from the same Manager that is inactive or delayed.
Observe whether write-data completion for the original transaction proceeds without waiting on the other transaction.
expected_results: All write data for the transaction can be provided independently of the other transaction state.
No cross-transaction dependency is required for write-data completion from that Manager.
feature_list.requirement_text: When a Manager issues a write request, it must be able to provide all write data for that transaction, without dependency on other transactions from that Manager.

feature_list.test_description: Verify a Manager that has issued a write request and all write data can accept all responses for that transaction without dependency on other transactions from that Manager.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires observable write completion and response acceptance at the Manager. Internal acceptance capability may only be inferred from interface behavior.
test_steps: Issue a write request and complete all write data from the Manager.
Hold another transaction from the same Manager inactive or unfinished.
Observe whether all responses for the completed write transaction are accepted without waiting on the other transaction.
expected_results: All responses for the transaction are accepted independently of other transactions from that Manager.
No cross-transaction dependency is required for response acceptance.
feature_list.requirement_text: When a Manager has issued a write request and all write data, it must be able to accept all responses for that transaction, without dependency on other transactions from that Manager.

feature_list.test_description: Verify a Manager that has issued a read request can accept all read data for that transaction without dependency on other transactions from that Manager.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires observable read-data acceptance at the Manager with more than one transaction context. Internal acceptance capability may only be inferred from interface behavior.
test_steps: Issue a read request from the Manager.
Create another transaction context from the same Manager that is inactive or incomplete.
Observe whether all read data for the issued read transaction is accepted without waiting on the other transaction.
expected_results: All read data for the transaction is accepted independently of other transactions from that Manager.
No cross-transaction dependency is required for read-data acceptance.
feature_list.requirement_text: When a Manager has issued a read request, it must be able to accept all read data for that transaction, without dependency on other transactions from that Manager.

feature_list.test_description: Trace permission for a Manager to wait for one transaction to complete before issuing another transaction request.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement is permission language, not a mandatory behavior. Edge case states concurrency is not mandatory, so only permissibility can be traced unless implementation behavior is defined.
test_steps: Identify whether the implementation allows serialized transaction issue by the Manager.
Confirm what observable condition defines transaction completion and subsequent issue eligibility.
Clarify implementation policy if verification of this permitted mode is required.
expected_results: The requirement is recognized as optional behavior rather than a mandatory concurrency rule.
Additional implementation-specific context is needed to verify the permitted serialized mode concretely.
feature_list.requirement_text: A Manager is permitted to wait for one transaction to complete before issuing another transaction request.

feature_list.test_description: Trace permission for a Subordinate to wait for one transaction to complete before accepting another request, giving credits, or sending transfers for another transaction.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requirement is permission language, not a mandatory behavior. Edge case states a fully serialized Subordinate is allowed, so only permissibility can be traced unless implementation behavior is defined.
test_steps: Identify whether the implementation allows a fully serialized Subordinate behavior across transactions.
Confirm what observable condition defines transaction completion and when cross-transaction activity may resume.
Clarify implementation policy if verification of this permitted mode is required.
expected_results: The requirement is recognized as optional behavior rather than a mandatory acceptance or concurrency rule.
Additional implementation-specific context is needed to verify the permitted serialized Subordinate mode concretely.
feature_list.requirement_text: A Subordinate is permitted to wait for one transaction to complete before accepting another request, giving credits or sending transfers for another transaction.

feature_list.test_description: Verify a Subordinate does not block acceptance of data-less write requests due to transactions with leading write data.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires a scenario with a data-less write request and another transaction containing leading write data, plus observable request acceptance behavior.
test_steps: Present a transaction with leading write data and, while it is active, issue a data-less write request to the Subordinate.
Observe acceptance of the data-less write request under that condition.
Check whether the active leading-write-data transaction prevents acceptance.
expected_results: The data-less write request is accepted despite transactions with leading write data being present.
Acceptance of the data-less write request is not blocked by the other transaction condition.
feature_list.requirement_text: A Subordinate must not block acceptance of data-less write requests due to transactions with leading write data.

feature_list.test_description: Trace the stated example condition where the write request channel contains more register stages than the write data channel.
feature_list.test_constraints: This is explanatory cause text, not a normative requirement. No mandatory behavior is defined by this statement alone.
test_steps: Identify the implementation pipeline depths of the write request and write data channels.
Determine whether the write request channel can have more register stages than the write data channel.
Link this condition to any separately specified required behavior, if applicable.
expected_results: The statement is treated as contextual explanatory text rather than a standalone measurable requirement.
No independent pass/fail functional verification can be completed from this text alone.
feature_list.requirement_text: This can occur if the write request channel contains more register stages than the write data channel.

feature_list.test_description: Verify the interconnect realigns the request and write data when it is required to determine the destination address space or Subordinate space.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires an implementation where the interconnect performs destination determination and observable alignment of request and write data. Exact alignment mechanism and observability are not specified.
test_steps: Create a write path scenario where the interconnect determines the destination address space or Subordinate space.
Drive a write request and corresponding write data through the interconnect.
Observe whether the request and write data are realigned before delivery toward the destination.
expected_results: The interconnect realigns the request and write data in the stated condition.
The delivered request and write data are aligned for the resolved destination path.
feature_list.requirement_text: When the interconnect is required to determine the destination address space or Subordinate space, it must realign the request and write data.

feature_list.test_description: Verify realignment causes write data to be signaled as valid only to the Subordinate for which it is destined.
feature_list.test_constraints: Requires observable destination-specific write-data validity signaling after realignment. The exact validity indication and destination tagging are not specified.
test_steps: Drive a write transaction through the realignment condition toward a resolved destination Subordinate.
Observe where write data validity is signaled after realignment.
Check that validity is asserted only on the destination Subordinate path for that write data.
expected_results: Write data is signaled as valid only to the destined Subordinate.
No non-destination Subordinate path observes valid write data for that transaction.
feature_list.requirement_text: This realignment is required to assure that the write data is signaled as being valid only to the Subordinate for which it is destined.
